data.title: Atea Pharmaceuticals announces share repurchase program
data.description:
• Atea Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:AVIR) announced on Thursday the appointment of Howard H. Berman to its board. 
  • Atea also announced that its board has authorized, and will promptly begin executing upon, the repurchase of up to $25M of the company’s common stock.
  • Atea Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:AVIR) shares were up more than 3% in premarket trading.

data.title: LVMH Moët Hennessy raises CEO retirement age to give Arnault another 9 years
data.description:
LVMH Moët Hennessy (OTCPK:LVMHF) (OTCPK:LVMUY) shareholders approved a change to the company’s bylaws that would raise the maximum age of chairman and CEO to 85 from 80, giving Bernard Arnault control of the luxury conglomerate for another 9 years.

This is the second time the maximum age was raised in the last three years.

Arnault has run the company since the late 80s, when he and two other associates merged Christian Dior with Louis Vuitton and Moët Hennessy, creating the iconic LVMH global brand.  Over the years, strategic and profitable acquisitions that included Tiffany’s, Bulgari, Givenchy, Fendi, and Sephora vaulted LVMH (OTCPK:LVMHF) (OTCPK:LVMUY) to become Europe’s largest company and made Arnault one of the richest men in the world.

And while his two eldest children hold executive positions at the company, Arnault has not yet named a successor.

Although the stock has suffered lately from geopolitical events and sluggish demand out of China, the company remains the largest luxury brand in the world, and a bellwether to the state of the global economy. Its most recent quarterly results, which showed an unexpected drop op in sales, rattled investors and reverberated through the luxury category.

LVMH (OTCPK:LVMHF) (OTCPK:LVMUY) shares are fractionally lower on the Paris stock exchange.

data.title: American Express Q1 earnings beat as NII rises, credit provision drops
data.description:
American Express (NYSE:AXP) Q1 earnings and revenue on Thursday beat the average analyst estimate, as its net interest income and card fee revenue rose as its provision for credit losses declined.

Its network volume, though, declined more than expected from the prior quarter. American Express (NYSE:AXP) stock dipped 1.3% in premarket trading.

The credit card lender and network now maintained its 2025 guidance of $15.00-$15.50 ($15.26 consensus) and 8%-10% revenue growth. 

Q1 total network volume of $439.6B, trailing the Visible Alpha consensus of $443.3B, dropped from $464.0B in Q4 and advanced from $419.2B in Q1 2024. Network volume increased 5% Y/Y vs. +7% Y/Y in the prior quarter. Total billed business increased 6% Y/Y to $387.4B, and compared with 8% growth in Q4.

Q1 GAAP EPS of $3.64, beating the $3.47 consensus, increased from $3.04 in Q4 2024 and $3.33 in last year’s Q1.

Q1 revenue was $17.0B, topping the $16.9B consensus, slipped from $17.2B in the previous quarter and climbed from $15.8B a year ago.

"Our performance across key areas, including Card Member spending, customer retention, demand for our premium products, and credit performance, continued to be strong across our customer base, consistent with and in many cases better than what we saw in 2024," said Chairman and CEO Stephen J. Squeri.

Net card fee revenue grew to $2.33B from $2.25B in Q4 and $1.97B in last years’ Q1.

American Express (AXP) acquired 3.4M new cards during the quarter, compared with 3.0M new cards in the prior quarter and 3.4M new cards in Q1 2024.

Q1 net interest income of $4.17B, vs. the Visible Alpha consensus of $4.10B, climbed from $4.04B in the previous quarter and $3.77B a year ago.

Provision for credit losses of $1.15B, vs.the Visible Alpha estimate of $1.38B, decreased from $1.29B in Q4 and $1.27 B in last year’s Q1.

Consolidated expenses of $12.5B, less than the VA estimate of $12.4B, fell from $13.1B in the prior quarter and increased from $11.4B a year ago.

Card member loans decreased to $139,.2B from $139.7 in the prior quarter and increased from $126.6B a year ago. Net write-off (principal, interest, and fees) was %, vs. 2.2% in Q4 and % in Q1 2024.

Conference call at 8:30 AM ET.

Earlier, American Express GAAP EPS of $3.64 beats by $0.17, revenue of $16.97B beats by $40M

data.title: VersaBank adds second US RPP partner
data.description:
• VersaBank (NASDAQ:VBNK) said on Thursday that through its wholly owned subsidiary, VersaBank USA National Association, it has  entered into an agreement with its second US Receivable Purchase Program partner under which the partner will leverage VersaBank's RPP to fund a portion of its loan and lease originations.
  • VersaBank also announced its US RPP portfolio has surpassed US$70 million in assets in only 75 days since adding its first partner US RPP partner on January 30, 2025. The bank is on target to achieve US$290 million in US RPP in fiscal 2025.
  • VersaBank also announced that it has significantly expanded its nationwide network of authorized deposit brokers in Canada with the addition of Bank of Montreal Nesbitt Burns. The addition of BMO Nesbitt Burns to the bank's deposit broker network is expected to further contribute to expansion of VersaBank's NIM in fiscal 2025.
  • The bank further announced that it remains on track to reach its target of $1 billion of commitments in Canada Mortgage and Housing Corporation by the end of fiscal 2025. 
  • VersaBank (NASDAQ:VBNK) shares were up 2.75% in premarket trading.

data.title: Earnings snapshot: Charles Schwab beats Q1 top-line and bottom-line estimates
Troubadour Resources appoints Zachary Kotowych as CEO
data.description:
• Troubadour Resources (OTCPK:TROUF) announced on Friday the appointment of Zachary Kotowych as chief executive officer, corporate secretary, and director of the company, effective immediately. 
  • The company said Kotowych has also been appointed as a member of the Company’s Audit Committee.
  • Kotowych previously served in corporate development at Abitibi Metals, a Quebec-focused exploration company advancing the high-grade B26 Polymetallic Deposit and the Beschefer Gold Project. 
  • Prior to joining Abitibi, Kotowych held equity research roles with Haywood Securities and Red Cloud Securities, where he covered mining equities. 
  • The company also announced the resignation of Christopher Huggins from his roles as chief executive officer, corporate secretary and director. 
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Trump slams Fed Chair Powell over delay in lowering interest rates, pushes for ouster
data.description:
President Donald Trump and Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell’s disagreement on economic policies seems to have escalated after the former said Powell's “termination cannot come fast enough.”

Trump, on his Truth Social platform, said the Fed should have lowered interest rates by now and argued that “USA is getting rich on tariffs.”

The comments come a day after Powell expressed concerns about the likely higher-than-anticipated effects from tariffs, including possibly putting pressure on the central bank's dual mandate of stable prices and strong employment.

Wall Street closed lower on Powell's comments on Wednesday.

Trump further added that the report Powell issued on Wednesday was “another, and typical, complete 'mess!'” and criticized the Fed Chair for a supposed delay in lowering interest rates.

data.title: State Street Non-GAAP EPS of $2.04 beats by $0.03, revenue of $3.28B misses by $40M
data.description:
• State Street press release (NYSE:STT): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $2.04 beats by $0.03.
  • Revenue of $3.28B (+4.5% Y/Y) misses by $40M.
  • AUC/A and AUM
    •Investment Servicing AUC/A as of quarter-end increased 6% to $46.7 trillion, mainly due to higher quarter-end market levels and flows
    •Investment Management AUM as of quarter-end increased 9% to $4.7 trillion, mainly driven by higher quarter-end market levels and net inflows

data.title: OpenAI's $500B Stargate venture mulls U.K. investment in future - report
data.description:
Stargate, a $500B U.S. datacenter project spearheaded by SoftBank Group (OTCPK:SFTBY) (OTCPK:SFTBF) and OpenAI, is mulling a future investment in the U.K. as it explores overseas locations to set up AI infrastructure, the Financial Times reported.

Britain has emerged as one of its top choices amid a recent push by the country's Prime Minister Keir Starmer’s to boost investment in the nation's growing AI industry, including a plan to bolster developers’ access to energy, the report added, citing people familiar with the matter.

Stargate's focus will remain on American infrastructure, but investment in the U.K. has been considered as part of a future international expansion if the project is successful, the report added.

Plans by the U.K. government to try to boost datacenter's access to electricity have attracted the project’s interest, the report noted.

Germany and France were also being seen as candidates for investment, according to the report. In February, France unveiled a €109B project in private investments in the AI ecosystem.

“As announced in January, Stargate remains fully committed to investing up to $500 billion over the next four years to build AI infrastructure in the United States," said OpenAI and SoftBank in a joint statement to Seeking Alpha.

Over the past few months, OpenAI has been lobbying governments in Europe to ease regulations and invest in data center infrastructure, the report noted.

Last week, the European Commission announced its plan to invest more in AI data centers and simplify rules around its AI Act.

In January, OpenAI CEO Sam Altman, SoftBank CEO Masayoshi Son and Oracle (NYSE:ORCL) Co-Founder and Chairman Larry Ellison joined U.S. President Donald Trump at the White House to officially announce the $500B four-year initiative to build out AI infrastructure. OpenAI had said then that it would begin deploying $100B immediately for the Stargate Project.

Earlier this month, OpenAI announced a $40B new funding round, which included SoftBank, that valued the AI startup at $300B.

Oracle did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

data.title: Blackstone Q1 earnings beat, as inflows rise
data.description:
Blackstone (NYSE:BX) Q1 earnings beat the Wall Street consensus as the private equity firm pulled in its highest levels of inflows in almost three years amid a volatile market. Assets under management climbed to $1.17T.

"We also delivered positive investment performance across all of our major strategies," said Chairman and CEO Stephen A. Schwarzman. "We are well positioned to navigate the current environment with $177B of dry powder to deploy and a resilient, capital-light business mode."

Its private equity infrastructure turned in the strongest performance of its strategies in the quarter.

Blackstone (NYSE:BX) stock rose 0.5% in Thursday premarket trading.

Net inflows of $61.6B compared with inflows of $57.5B in the prior quarter. 

Total assets under management of $1.17T at March, 31, 2025, topping the Visible Alpha consensus of $1.15T, rose from $1.13T at Dec. 31, 2024. Fee-earning AUM increased to $860.1B from $830.7B at the end of Q4 2024.

Q1 distributable EPS of $1.09, beating the consensus of $1.06, dropped from $1.69 in Q4 2024 and increased from $0.98 in last year’s Q1.

Q1 fee-related earnings of $1.26B, or $1.03 per share, beating the Visible Alpha consensus of $0.85, rose from $1.18B, or $0.96 per share, in Q4 and $1.16B, or $0.95 per share, in last year’s Q1.

Net management and advisory fees were $1.89B, up from $1.86B in the previous quarter and $1.71B a year ago.

At the end of the quarter, Blackstone (BX) had undrawn capital for investment, or dry powder, of $177.2B vs. $168.6B at the end of Q4. During the first quarter of 2025, the company deployed $36.4B of capital and recorded realizations of $25.5B.

Total expenses of $1.89B increased from $1.50B in Q4 and $1.79B in Q1 2024. Compensation and benefit expenses climbed to $1.43B from $1.04B in the prior quarter and $1.31B a year ago.

Q1 investment performance by segment (appreciation/gross returns):

  • Real Estate

    • Opportunistic: +0.2%

    • Core: +1.2% 

  • Private Equity
    • Corporate Private Equity: +1.1%

    • Tactical Opportunities: +2.9%

    • Secondaries: +0.6%

    • Infrastructure: +7.5%

  • Credit & Insurance

    • Private Credit: +2.7%

    • Liquid Credit: +0.5%

  • Multi-Asset Investing

    • Absolute Return Composite:+2.6%

Conference call at 9:00 AM ET.

Earlier, Blackstone Non-GAAP EPS of $1.09 beats by $0.03, revenue of $3.29B beats by $570M
